words.meaning: to use something well or use someone unfairly
words.example: Some companies exploit legal gaps to avoid taxes.

words.meaning: to promise that something will happen or work
words.example: The shop will guarantee repairs for one year.

words.meaning: to say firmly that something is true or necessary
words.example: Parents insist that the crossing needs a traffic light.

words.meaning: to fill or use a place, time, or position
words.example: The meeting will occupy the main hall all morning.

words.meaning: to need to pay or give something to someone
words.example: The company still owes workers two weeks of pay.
Firefighters rescue people from flooded homes.

words.meaning: to stop working because of age or choice
words.example: Many people retire later than before.

words.meaning: to meet a need, rule, or wish
words.example: The plan must satisfy safety requirements.

words.meaning: to use one thing instead of another
words.example: You can substitute yogurt for cream in this recipe.

words.meaning: to warn that you may hurt someone or cause harm
words.example: Rising costs threaten small local businesses.
